fleet_enable_deny._comment: Cause-to-fire deny set §3.3 (Q-HA-CONFIGWRITE). Hard-denied at both service-call and config-write executors. Returns FLEET_ENABLE_DENY sentinel — distinct from Tier C — for precise audit reason. Forms 1-6 by (domain,service); form 7 as homeassistant.turn_on|toggle + automation/script-target case.

domain_service.reason: fires disabled automation action sequence (skip_condition defaults true) — the v2-missed killer
toggling a stopped script starts (runs) it — the other v2-missed form
named-script service form (script.<object_id>) runs the script; wildcard catches all remaining script.* services

homeassistant_wildcard.reason: homeassistant.turn_on/toggle resolving to automation.*/script.* target (generic-domain enable caught by entity domain-prefix, not service domain)

disable_scope._comment: Disable-scope rules §3.4 (Q-HA-CONFIGWRITE). W1 = rule data only; W2 wires executor enforcement logic. Governs fleet-initiated disable/delete/overwrite of pre-existing automations and scripts.

hard_deny_ops_if_prior_has_critical.description: HARD-DENIED: delete, disable (turn_off), or overwrite (upsert) of a pre-existing automation/script whose prior body references any Tier-C/Critical entity. Prevents laundering a safety-interlock neuter through a single benign confirm.

permitted_own_deploy_backstop.description: Executor may force-disable the object it is currently deploying (initial_state:false + turn_off backstop per §3.1). Allowed ONLY when prior==null (genuine create) or prior body references no Critical entity. Never against a pre-existing Critical interlock — that upsert is hard-denied at step 4 before any backstop runs.

permitted_fleet_turn_off.description: Fleet-initiated turn_off on an automation/script whose prior body (GET-first) references no Tier-C/Critical entity → Tier-B confirm required.
